name: M9 minimal media with custom trace element solution (PMC5888115)
description: M9 minimal media with custom trace element solution — a minimal medium extracted from Kim et al. (2018). Mapped to BiGG from the paper's stated composition.

provenance.citation: Kim et al. Systems assessment of transcriptional regulation on central carbon metabolism by Cra and CRP. Nucleic Acids Research (2018). DOI: 10.1093/nar/gky069. [PMC5888115]
provenance.notes: Extracted from the primary paper (snippet: M9 minimal media was also supplemented with 1 ml trace element solution (100×) containing 1 g EDTA, 29 mg ZnSO4·7H2O, 198 mg MnCl2·4H2O, 254 mg CoCl2·6H2O, 13.4 mg CuCl2 and 147 mg CaCl2.). Component names mapped to BiGG (see mapping_confidence); presence-based bounds unless the paper gave exchange bounds; concentration_mM where units allowed. Extraction confidence: high.
provenance.verification: paper-verified (complete recipe; cited + supplementary sources chased)
provenance.base_expansion: Base 'M9' expanded to standard composition: M9 minimal medium (Sambrook & Russell 2001): Na2HPO4, KH2PO4, NaCl, NH4Cl, MgSO4, CaCl2 + carbon source.
provenance.verification_evidence: M9 minimal media with different carbon sources, glucose, fructose, galactose, succinate, glycerol or acetate. The concentration of carbon sources was 0.2% (w/v).
provenance.base_medium: M9 minimal medium (standard) + custom EDTA-chelated trace element solution

sources_chased.what: M9 base salt composition (paper names 'M9 minimal media' with no citation)
sources_chased.how: canonical M9 formulation (CSH/Sambrook); paper gives no modified-M9 reference
stated verbatim in Materials and Methods

recuration.notes: Generic record for the M9 + custom trace element solution (carbon source shown as acetate to match the target's current components). Aerobic (37C, agitation). Trace solution has NO iron and standard M9 adds none, so prior Fe2+/Fe3+ entries are unsupported and dropped.
